TextENGNew: Monday, October 11th, 1982. (Public holiday)
"...Haah~, finally made it."
Tomoe stepped out of the car and stretched with a groan.
After circling the town searching in vain for parking, she had finally discovered a cramped temporary lot tucked into a corner of the shopping district near the station.
Being a rural town, she could see cars of all sizes parked illegally along the roadsides here and there.
Enforcement was apparently quite lax here... but still, a detective could hardly take the lead in flouting traffic regulations.
(The car parked next to mine was a foreign model though, which was a bit intimidating.)
The street outside the lot was lined with hostess clubs and bars, so the foreign car most likely belonged to someone from the 'underworld.'
A detective's car parked right beside a gangster's. What a combination.
"Still, that was quite a drive from Kakiuchi.@k@vS11/28/420100483. ...Maybe I should have taken the train after all."
She had good reason to grumble — the roads after leaving the highway were narrow and winding, and she hadn't been able to relax for a single moment.
At one particularly harrowing point, she had pressed the accelerator thinking she was on a straightaway, only to be met with a sudden hairpin turn.
...A chill ran down her spine as she recalled how, just a short while ago, she had very nearly smashed through the guardrail and plunged to the bottom of the valley.
(Do the locals really have no trouble with roads like that?)

She recalled something the regional affairs staff had mentioned the other day. In Kakiuchi City over the past several years, there had been a noticeable increase in households relocating from other prefectures.
Of those, a particularly large number had apparently come from Okinomiya and the neighboring village of Hinamizawa...
After paying the lot attendant, she stepped outside and took in the view of the town.
The area around the station was more developed than she had expected — livelier than her grandmother's town in Ibaraki.
A housewife on her bicycle rang her bell with a cheerful trill as she pedaled past Tomoe on the way home from shopping.
Just around the next corner, a group of children in baseball uniforms came tumbling into view, chattering excitedly as they ran right past her.
"We did great today~!@k@vS11/00/VT3B_boyA2002. The pitcher on the other team must be so sore!"
"You even caught that last fly ball when we were really in a pinch. Nice job~!"
"Let's go just as hard next week!"
Their faces smeared with dirt and sweat but beaming with smiles, the children — apparently on their way home from a game — went happily on their way.
Tomoe found herself smiling along with them, watching their small retreating backs for a while before turning and continuing on.
...But just then.
Tomoe suddenly came to a halt when she heard a loud noise coming from the direction she had just left.
Something nagged at her, and she retraced her steps to investigate.
A short way down the alley there was a vacant lot, and in front of it three motorcycles lay toppled on the ground like fallen dominoes.
"Whad ya doooooo!!@k@vS11/00/VT3B_huryoA2002. Knocken' em oveeer!!"
"Graaaah, no waaaay!!"
"Oaaaahh!!@k@vS11/00/VT3B_huryoC2002. Whatcha gonna do bout iiiiiiit?!"
"Ah, umm... I-I'm sorr...!"
And right next to where the motorcycles fell, there were three young male delinquents threateningly surrounding one other person.
It was hard to see around the backs of the boys, but the fragile trembling voice sounded like it was coming from a girl.
"I-I was just looking the other way, and lightly tapped it with my elbow...@k@vS11/36/420600003. I-I'm sorry, I'm sorry!!"
"Lightly tapped it with yer elboooow!!@k@vS11/00/VT3B_huryoA2004. Them bikes're worth more than our lives, ya think ya can just apologiiize?!!"
"U-uh, umm... well...!!"
The girl's voice was getting smaller and smaller, and Tomoe could hear sobbing mixed in.
As soon as she heard that, Tomoe let out a sigh and stepped into the alley.
She could see the girl's face in the gap between the boys.
The girl with her hair tied in buns on both sides had a terrified, tear-filled look in her eyes.
"Uraah, how couldja knock em over?!@k@vS11/00/VT3B_huryoB2003. Huuh?!"
"Instead of apologizing with yer mouth, show us something to prove yer sincerity!!"
"Huuuh? I can't hear ha!@k@vS11/00/VT3B_huryoA2006. ...If ya got no money, show come compassion! Go out on a date with us... yeaaah?!"
""Geh hya hya...!""
(...Come on, how old are these delinquents?)
Seeing the situation unfold with no context, Tomoe was more exasperated than enraged. She shook her head.
...At least back in Tokyo, these stereotypical delinquents had gone completely extinct.
Moreover, the three of them were ganging up on a single girl... It was well beyond cowardly, truly deplorable.
The thugs that Tomoe knew were better than that. They had an unwritten rule not to be violent toward women...
"...Aah, please forgive me...!@k@vS11/36/420600008. I-I'm begging you...!"
"Let's go play fer a li'l while!@k@vS11/00/VT3B_huryoA2009. ...On a date, yeah?"
"I know a good spot... ya got time, right?"
"S-someone... h-help...!"
"Heey, why so scared?@k@vS11/00/VT3B_huryoC2006. I'm talkin' to ya, are ya listenin'?"
"...Ah, that's no good.@k@vS11/28/420100489. With an invitation like that, how can you expect a girl to do anything but turn around immediately?"
""...Huuuuh?!""
The boys turned back toward Tomoe all at once. They looked so pissed off they were ready to chop someone to bits.
...But they regained their composure as soon as they realized their enemy was a lady. Their expressions warped into nasty smiles.
The girl who was surrounded... had a relieved "I'm saved" expression on her face as she anxiously watched on.
(Well... that's expected.)
They think some reckless woman passing by stepped in to help the girl. ...They probably think she's some sucker armed with a leek.
"What's that, lady?@k@vS11/00/VT3B_huryoA2012. You wanna come play with us too?"
"Play... with who?@k@vS11/28/420100492. And where?"
"Ain't it obvious?@k@vS11/00/VT3B_huryoB2008. Wanna come with us for somethin' good?"
"Got some free time? ...We'll keep you entertained all night!"
"Gehhehehehe... How about it, lady?@k@vS11/00/VT3B_huryoB2010. We won't do anything bad!"
(...What's a suitable retort for this fully loaded invitation?)
She thought for "about two seconds" for something emotionally scathing, and was planning to say something about their "disadvantage"... but that seemed less "relevant" than expected.
And so, Tomoe stared at them with a fake smile and a disgusted look on her face. Then,.
"Idiots.@k@vS11/28/420100495. That invitation is 4.8 billion years too early.@k@vS11/28/420100496. Go back to the creation of the Earth and start over."
"Dat so?"
"I dun like the tone of this brat!@k@vS11/00/VT3B_huryoB2012. An her face looks like a man's!!"
*pop*.
Tomoe was trying to keep things peaceful, but that once sentence drove her over the edge, all the way to "send them to Hell."
"Whats wit you?! Huh...?"
"...You know.@k@vS11/28/420100499. I forgot to mention, there are a few choice words you shouldn't say around me."
"Hurgh?!"

"The first is "brat."@k@vS11/28/420100501. Saying I look young is fine, but saying "brat" drives me absolutely mad.@k@vS11/28/420100502. Maybe I don't act like it, but I'm really conscious of my childish face...!!"
"The next word is "man!"@k@vS11/28/420100504. This is even more infuriating than the previous one. So I won't forgive you, no matter what you say or do!!"
"What about me looks like a man?@k@vS11/28/420100506. My chest?@k@vS11/28/420100507. My height?@k@vS11/28/420100508. How obnoxious! Either way, it's none of your business!!"
"That's why I give everyone who talks about my life that way@k@vS11/28/420100510. a one way ticket straight to the afterlife!!"
"Gh, gyaaahh!!"
"...Normally, when trying to "intimidate" someone you have to be more calm."
"Hii, hiiii?!"
"Now, one last question."
"...Do you guys have life insuraaance?!"
Tomoe decided that was enough "punishment" for these delinquents, so she let them make their escape and watched them go with a huge smile on her face.
...She was carrying an extendable baton in her jacket pocket just in case of emergency, but it apparently wasn't even necessary.
For the time being, She turned back toward the girl who had been absentmindedly watching that "punishment" for several minutes now. She smiled at the girl and tried talking to her.
"...Are you all right?"
...She didn't appear hurt, so apparently they were only threatening her with harsh language. Tomoe heaved a sigh of relief after verifying that.
"...Uh, umm... there's blood on your hand."
"Hmm? ...Aah, it's all right.@k@vS11/28/420100516. It's not my blood."
Tomoe smiled at the girl while wiping it away with a wet tissue.
...It was a bit painful, but she'd held back, so it wasn't broken... She thought. Probably.
"W-wow, you're tough..."
"Ah, well.@k@vS11/28/420100518. I did make it to stage three in kendo."
"R-really...?"
"...You have to step in, even if you're unrelated.@k@vS11/28/420100520. It's too bad I didn't bring my bamboo sword."
Was there something weird about that answer? ...The girl burst out into tearful laughter. Her laugh was positively adorable, so Tomoe laughed in spite of herself along with her.
(See, there are people who smile and laugh with me.)
Tomoe pictured the face of another person with no sense of humor, and cursed them in her mind.
"Uh... thank you so much for helping me.@k@vS11/36/420600019. I, umm..."
"It's no big deal.@k@vS11/28/420100523. ...But it's dangerous for a lone girl to be walking down an alley like this.@k@vS11/28/420100524. You should be more careful in the future."
"Oh, right.@k@vS11/36/420600021. I was just trying to take a little shortcut..."
The girl hung her head and sounded terribly apologetic as she said that.
Thinking that she was speaking a bit too much like a guardian, Tomoe cleared her throat and then continued in a jovial tone.

"Anyway, when I first heard their voices I started to wonder if a gorilla had escaped from the zoo.@k@vS11/28/420100526. People like that are an endangered species in the city.@k@vS11/28/420100527. ...Are those guys from this town?"
"I think... they're probably from somewhere else.@k@vS11/36/420600023. I've... never seen people like them in town before."
That moment.
As soon as she heard the word "city," the girl's expression stiffened.
"...So, are you a local?"
"Oh, no.@k@vS11/36/420600025. ...I moved away from here back in the spring, and I'm visiting today."
"I see. It's a holiday weekend, isn't it?"
(Actually, I'm supposed to be visiting my old home too.)
Thanks to this, she had to disappoint her grandmother.
...Although, her old-fashioned grandmother would encourage her to be doing something she enjoys rather than relaxing at home.
Suddenly, She looked down at the collapsed motorcycles, crouched down and took note of their license plates.
They were from another prefecture, just as the girl had suggested.
Not only that, but the cover under the keyhole was broken. ...Probably stolen.
When she saw that, she pulled the notebook out of her breast pocket and quickly wrote down the license plate numbers.
"What's it?"
"Are you... a police officer?"
"Yes, that's right.@k@vS11/28/420100534. ...Although, I'm on vacation today."
As soon as she found out Tomoe was with the police, the girl's face suddenly turned nervous. ...It was a typical reaction, but it was a bit disheartening considering she had just helped the girl.
"...Are you close to home?@k@vS11/28/420100536. If you're scared, I can walk with you partway there."
"No, I'm all right.@k@vS11/36/420600030. ...I'm really sorry."
"Ah, wait..."
The girl began walking away with her head down, but Tomoe suddenly called out to her.
Then, she thought about asking the girl just for her name... but stopped herself.
Nobody really enjoys giving their name to the police.
So, instead...
"My name is Minai.@k@vS11/28/420100539. It's written with the kanji for south and well."
"If you run into any trouble, don't hesitate to contact me.@k@vS11/28/420100541. ...I'll do everything in my power to help."
Her face was backlit, so Tomoe couldn't make out her expression very well, but the girl bowed her head once more and then quickly left the area.
Even after she went out of view, Tomoe stopped and stood there for a while...
...The meeting spot was a fairly secluded coffee shop.
"...This is it, huh?"
When she opened the door, she heard the characteristic ringing of the bell in the coffee shop.
She felt the strong scent of cigarette smoke the moment she entered, and couldn't help but grimace.
"Ohh, Tomoe-chan. Over here~."
A hand like a log was conspicuously waving at her from a table a short distance away.
Though acting aloof, his facial expression had a crafty sharpness to it. ...It was Detective Ooishi from Okinomiya Station.
"It's been a long time, Ooishi-san.@k@vS11/28/420100544. Thank you for making time for this."
"Oh it's no big deal. Long time no see, Tomoe-chan!@k@vS11/11/420300025. It's been so long. Has your chest gotten a little bigger since we last met?"
"...It's only been a few months since we last met, you know?@k@vS11/28/420100546. They're not like balloons. They don't change that quickly."
"Nfu fu!@k@vS11/11/420300027. But there are convenient solutions for that nowadays.@k@vS11/11/420300028. Push up bras, cosmetic surgery..."
"I'm not rich enough to throw money at something like that!@k@vS11/28/420100548. ...If you say another word, I'm going to file a sexual harassment claim, got it?"
"Na ha ha!!"
Ooishi's large belly shook as he let out a booming laugh.
...It was as if he started talking like that more and more as Tomoe got older.
As this point in time, the ratio of her respect and disdain for him were close to reaching the tipping point... It was reprehensible.
"Well first things first. Let's order something.@k@vS11/11/420300031. The parfait here is really good.@k@vS11/11/420300032. ...Ah, I'll have a second helping of this."
"Actually, today sweets are a bit...@k@vS11/28/420100550. Oh, I'll have it hot."
For now, Tomoe submitted an order then turned to have a look around.
There were no customers in the shop. It was quiet enough that she could hear the baseball game on the TV in the counter area, some distance away from their seat.
"...Here you go."
"Ah, thanks."
The host brought over the coffee she ordered, then turned and headed back to the counter area. After confirming that, Tomoe leaned forward and spoke to Ooishi in a hushed voice.
"...So, about what we discussed on the phone.@k@vS11/28/420100553. I'd like to ask for a little help, Ooishi-san."

"Nfu fu!@k@vS11/11/420300034. If there's anything I can do to help, please let me know.@k@vS11/11/420300035. Lending a hand to an up and coming police officer in her time of need is an attractive proposition~!@k@vS11/11/420300036. However, my cooperation fee is a bit steep, you know?"
"...Three bottles of "Kashima Sakura."@k@vS11/28/420100555. High-grade, pure rice wine. A gift from the chief brewer at the first storehouse."
"Ohhh!@k@vS11/11/420300038. Wonderful, Tomoe-chan, you know me so well~! Na ha ha!"
...She was actually planning to give it to him anyway, so it was no additional expense. Although, Ooishi probably understood that as well.
"Alright, ask away.@k@vS11/11/420300040. What did you want to discuss about this case?"
"Very well. First, take a look at this..."
She pulled out the files she had secretly borrowed from the Ibaraki prefectural police department and laid them out on the table, explaining them to Ooishi one by one.
Ooishi burned through five cigarettes while silently reading through everything and listening to her story, then finally spoke up.
"...So?@k@vS11/11/420300042. What did the boy's girlfriend have to say?"

"She said if it wasn't suicide... then the killer must be Reina Ryuugu.@k@vS11/28/420100558. Did she curse him to death, or did she kill him more directly?@k@vS11/28/420100559. ...Although, in both cases the probability is close to zero."
If it was the latter, there was no way she could've avoided being seen at the hospital. And more importantly, Reina Ryuugu didn't seem to have a reason to kill him to begin with.
...And the former was utter nonsense. If it was possible to kill someone with a curse, there would be no way to catch evildoers.
With that in mind, Tomoe wasn't just skeptical of supernatural phenomena, but outright denied it.
"The curse of Oyashiro-sama, huh?"

"...She asked him about that on an earlier visit, but even the victim himself claimed to have no knowledge of that before ultimately jumping to his death.@k@vS11/11/420300045. ...But it feels too odd to write it off as a mere accident or suicide."

"The first section chief at the prefectural police department shares the same opinion that it's likely murder.@k@vS11/28/420100561. ...This is essentially an unauthorized investigation, but I'm acquainted with this girl, so at the very least I'd like to understand as much as you know."
"Oh my, that's a fine line of reasoning.@k@vS11/11/420300047. ...Aah, please don't think I'm saying that ironically.@k@vS11/11/420300048. Because I do seriously believe that."
"Oh, no. Not at all..."
Tomoe scratched her head as she said that.
For some reason, these days she had trouble accepting praise from other people. Her tendency to doubt people was a bad trend.

"Besides, it was my request that got you involved with this kid to begin with, Tomoe-chan.@k@vS11/11/420300050. So you can't say I'm unrelated to this case.@k@vS11/11/420300051. I'd be glad to help you out."
"Thank you very much.@k@vS11/28/420100564. Since I'm not from this area, there are limits on what I can do while investigating in this town..."
"Nfu fu, that shouldn't be a problem.@k@vS11/11/420300053. Isn't that the point of your wide area investigator title?"
"Well, that's true, but even so..."
When Ooishi pointed that out, Tomoe responded with a vague, forced smile.
Even with her wide area investigator title, cases like this that crossed between the Kanto and Chubu regions still caused problems.
So, Tomoe's intention was to hand things off to Ibaraki prefectural police after finding some clues.

"But just throwing it away half-complete leaves a bad taste in my mouth.@k@vS11/28/420100567. I do understand that this is for my own self-satisfaction though. So long as the case is solved I'll be satisfied."
"Selfless as ever, eh?@k@vS11/11/420300055. Aren't employees on the career path supposed to seek a steady flow of achievements in the name of career advancement?"

"I'm on the "quasi" career path.@k@vS11/28/420100569. ...Besides, if my involvement on this case became widely known, the only thing that would increase is the number of written apologies I'd have to write.@k@vS11/28/420100570. It may cause trouble for other people too..."
"Such a generous personality... Nfu fu!"
Ooishi smiled as he said that. ...Behind those words was a concerned old man thinking, "Then you could just ignore all this in that case."
Indeed, if she kept quiet and let it slide on account of it being outside her jurisdiction, that would be the end of the story.
It wasn't Tomoe's fault that Kouhei Sawamura died, nor was she particularly close to his girlfriend Nagisa.

Besides, the relevant precinct gave an evasive answer saying it was a falling accident, but Honjou was going to escalate it to prefectural police headquarters to take the investigation more seriously.
Even without Tomoe's involvement, the situation was slowly turning around.
(But... I can't just leave it all to everyone else.@k@vS11/28/420100572. Because I made a promise...)
That thought was probably the main reason Tomoe insisted on being so selfless.
But Ooishi could never resent her for that. In fact, deep down he respected it, and thought she was dependable...

"Anyway... what's the deal with "Oyashiro-sama?"@k@vS11/28/420100574. You didn't seem very concerned when I asked you about that during the previous investigation, Ooishi-san... but what does it mean?"

"Aah, I haven't given you a proper explanation about that, Tomoe-chan.@k@vS11/11/420300058. "Oyashiro-sama" is the guardian deity from the traditions passed down in Hinamizawa village... A calamitous deity."
"Calamitous deity...?@k@vS11/28/420100576. As in, an entity fated to bring disaster to the people?"
"Yes, precisely.@k@vS11/11/420300060. And "Oyashiro-sama" has ruled over the villagers of Hinamizawa with awe and fear since ancient times, when the village went by the name Onigafuchi..."
...Then, Ooishi lit a cigarette and began telling an old tale in a hushed tone.
"They say that long ago, humans and demons lived together in Hinamizawa."

"And when those demons descended upon the lower realm, they would occasionally kidnap and feed on humans.@k@vS11/11/420300063. ...This "Oyashiro-sama" acted as a mediator between the humans and demons, allowing them to coexist."
"That sounds like a benevolent god.@k@vS11/28/420100578. So why do you call it a calamitous god?"

"Even though the demons cohabitated with the humans, they couldn't cease their traditional custom of feeding on humans.@k@vS11/11/420300065. So, Oyashiro-sama agreed to forgive this behavior under a certain condition.@k@vS11/11/420300066. What you might call a sacrifice."
"The humans had their misgivings, but they accepted it because it was better than being killed indiscriminately."
That was the origin of the "curse."
In other words, Oyashiro-sama selected those of little faith and made them sacrifices to the "curse" by making them targets for the demon hunt.
And in response, the demons graciously devoured the pathetic humans selected for the sacrifice...
In other words, those who turn a blind eye to Oyashiro-sama have their lives taken at the hands of the demons.
"...This Oyashiro-sama sounds like a mafia boss.@k@vS11/28/420100580. Being killed or spared at the whim of some god?"

"Na ha ha, indeed.@k@vS11/11/420300069. But because of that, the people are afraid that turning away from Oyashiro-sama would put their lives in danger, so they continue to be faithful and respectful."
"And that is the root of the ancient faith in Oyashiro-sama, which still continues in Hinamizawa even today."

"But... if that's the case, why did the humans continue to live in Hinamizawa even after the threat of the demons continued?@k@vS11/28/420100582. Wouldn't it be better to migrate to another land in order to escape the danger?"
"Well, I don't know all the details, but I think that the land in Hinamizawa was easy to live comfortably in.@k@vS11/11/420300072. You know what they say, "Tyranny is more deadly than a tiger.""
Indeed, there was an old proverb that said even if it was dangerous to live near ferocious beasts, that was still preferable to heavy taxes and terrible work conditions.
She didn't know exactly what kind of forces were at work in the surrounding municipalities, but Onigafuchi village may very well have been a region free of conflict and tyranny.

"In addition to that, there are stories saying the demons possessed knowledge that the humans lacked, and offered to share food and drugs that humans couldn't make.@k@vS11/11/420300074. So, there was undoubtedly merit to their coexistence."

"I see.@k@vS11/28/420100584. ...But even though the demons were given the power to hunt humans, they chose to obey a human god.@k@vS11/28/420100585. Normally, you'd think they would ignore it and go around rampaging as they please."

"Oh no, in reality Oyashiro-sama was a demon as well.@k@vS11/11/420300076. And by actively engaging with the humans, Oyashiro-sama was able to garner the trust and fear necessary to act as an intermediary between humans and demons."

"In fact, the humans and demons co-mingled so closely, that their blood mixed after that.@k@vS11/11/420300078. And so, even today, legends persist that the denizens of Hinamizawa possess the blood of demons."
And it is said that the blood ran strongest in Hinamizawa's ancient ruling parties, the "Three Families." The Kimiyoshi, Sonozaki, and Furude families.
They had a powerful voice in the politics and rituals throughout the village, and they were able to lead the villagers to fight against the state in the campaign against the dam construction project.
"...In other words, the demons weren't actually monsters, but some sort of mountain dwelling race of hunters."

"Setting aside the cannibalism, these people with supernatural customs descended from the mountains and invaded the nearby agricultural community, but eventually found a way to coexist via conquest or peace."
"Ultimately, Oyashiro-sama was a charismatic leader who found common ground between the two sides... or something along those lines."
"Nfu fu. Tomoe-chan, you're such a realist.@k@vS11/11/420300080. We can't even have a conversation about gods and demons without you adding a cultural anthropological interpretation?"

"Oh, sorry.@k@vS11/28/420100590. But I simply can't believe in curses or other supernatural phenomena.@k@vS11/28/420100591. So saying that the curse of Oyashiro-sama is killing people just doesn't sit right with me."
Tomoe thought the idea of using a curse to subjugate people was utter nonsense to begin with.
If spells and magic could control the fate of men, even life and death, then there was no point in the very existence of the police, or law itself.
...Even as she thought that, she believed it to be an inelegant way of thinking. However...
"...Ooishi-san?"
Tomoe couldn't help but tilt her head and stare when she noticed Ooishi's oddly mysterious expression.
He thought silently for a while, carefully choosing his words... before finally addressing Tomoe.
"Tomoe-chan.@k@vS11/11/420300083. ...In this case, talk of a curse killing people isn't so ridiculous.@k@vS11/11/420300084. People really have died... even as recently as a few months ago."
People dying from a curse. It's not just some old tale, but something still happening today...?
Although, honestly... Tomoe was even more dubious than surprised.
However, Ooishi wasn't one to tell ghost stories or joke around intending to scare someone, and he began talking in a matter-of-fact tone.
"About the tradition I mentioned earlier, where the demons feasted on humans... In those days, the village held a yearly ceremony called "Watanagashi" where a sacrifice was offered to the demons."

"The humans selected for the sacrifice would have their stomach split open... and their entrails would be sent drifting down the river to quell the curse.@k@vS11/11/420300087. And that ceremony is still conducted in Hinamizawa, even today..."
"...@k@vS11/28/420100595.I find that hard to believe. Are you saying they're actually doing that?"

"Well, of course, they're not actually sending entrails drifting down the river.@k@vS11/11/420300089. Nowadays, they take some everyday product like a futon or a kimono... and slice out the cotton, then send that drifting downstream as a way of expressing their gratitude for their daily necessities."
"...Thank goodness."
Tomoe was relieved to hear that.
Even if it was a small, remote town deep in the mountains, she couldn't accept that such an inhumane act was being conducted out in the open.
"However... in lieu of that,@k@vS11/11/420300091. there have been mysterious incidents happening on the night of the Watanagashi festival in recent years."
"Mysterious... incidents?"
"Murders. And... missing people, too.@k@vS11/11/420300093. ...This is no joke. It's all true."
...Three years ago, the dam construction manager's dismembered body was discovered.
One of the people involved in the murder ran away, and was still missing.
Two years ago, a man who went against the village's wishes and accepted aid money from the state for supporting the dam construction project died in a falling accident.
His wife allegedly fell off the cliff together with him, but her whereabouts were still unknown.

Last year, the shrine priest and head of the Furude family, one of the Three Families ruling the area, who had taken a neutral stance in the campaign against the dam project, suddenly died of an unknown illness.
And his wife seemingly committed suicide by jumping into a nearby swamp. A will was found, but her body was never discovered.
And then...
"And this year as well, one person died... and another went missing."
...That happened on a Sunday in June.

A housewife was murdered late at night in the outskirts of the village, shortly after the village "Watanagashi" festival was held. She was the sister-in-law of the dam supporter who died two years ago.

"By the way, I didn't tell you this when you were commissioned for the Reina Ryuugu case.@k@vS11/11/420300096. Her face was crushed using some sort of blunt weapon.@k@vS11/11/420300097. ...Apparently the killer felt that level of contempt for the victim."

"Originally, there was a peculiar legend about this Watanagashi.@k@vS11/11/420300099. ...On the night of the festival, one person is killed and one goes missing.@k@vS11/11/420300100. ...That's what they say.@k@vS11/11/420300101. And it's repeating, even this year..."
"So, a person went missing too?"
"That's right. It was the son of the murdered housewife.@k@vS11/11/420300103. ...No, sorry, he's actually her nephew.@k@vS11/11/420300104. He suddenly went missing a few days after the incident..."
"...@k@vS11/28/420100602.Was this boy involved in the incident?"
"Involved?"
"For example, how did he feel toward the victim? Did he have an alibi for the day of the incident...?"
As soon as Ooishi heard that, he let out a light "hoh" as if impressed.
...In a murder case, it's typical for someone who disappears right after the incident to be considered a suspect.
However, thanks to the pattern of deaths from the "curse," ordinary people would regard the missing person as another "victim."
So, being able to identify him as a potential culprit based on that basic information was a testament to her excellent organizational skills.
"Oh, that's some impressive intuition.@k@vS11/11/420300107. ...Indeed, we did consider him a key suspect in the housewife's murder."
"First off, there were several suspicious points about his alibi."

"On top of that, there was some discord within the family. And he was exhibiting suspicious behavior just before the incident.@k@vS11/11/420300110. So even though we let him walk free, we still followed that lead..."
"But he ran away before investigators could apprehend him.@k@vS11/28/420100605. ...So, where did he go?"

"A witness testified seeing a person matching his description at a train station, but... in the end, it amounted to nothing.@k@vS11/11/420300112. Instead, someone suddenly came out claiming to be the real culprit shortly after that... so the investigation halted right there."
"Who was that?"
"Some weird drug addict that wasn't right in the head.@k@vS11/11/420300114. ...Although, he committed suicide during the interrogation, so the details aren't all that clear."
"Was he really the culprit?"
"That, I don't know.@k@vS11/11/420300116. The prefectural police seem to think so."
"...But the circumstances and motive are still unclear, and something doesn't smell right about how suddenly he appeared. It's all hard to believe."
"Even now, I think the nephew that disappeared is the culprit.@k@vS11/11/420300119. That's my firm belief."
"Also..."
"Also?"
"This might just be my detective's intuition talking to me, but...@k@vS11/11/420300122. I don't think the nephew committed this murder simply because of a domestic dispute."
"Originally, he was a kind-hearted boy who cared about his sister, and not predisposed to violence."
"So, if you'll indulge a selfish fantasy of mine, I think it was someone else's intention that he murder her and then disappear...@k@vS11/11/420300125. That's how I feel."
"Someone else's intention...? What do you mean?"
"In short, it's just like the story I told you earlier.@k@vS11/11/420300127. "Oyashiro-sama"... was the one who forced him to act."
"No way..."
Tomoe couldn't say it was impossible.
In fact, as she uttered the name, she thought of the girl who resorted to violence for some unknown reason...!
"So, that goes for Reina Ryuugu too? When she attacked Kouhei Sawamura...?"

"Well, there's no concrete evidence... but I don't think it's impossible.@k@vS11/11/420300129. I still don't know if "Oyashiro-sama" is literally a supernatural entity, or someone vocalizing the collective will of the village."
"However, that will can drive normal people living otherwise peaceful lives to their deaths, or to commit murder.@k@vS11/11/420300131. ...Can something with that sort of power truly exist?"
Drive someone to murder...?
So, was the previous incident between Reina Ryuugu and her classmates instigated by someone else's will...?
If so, what on earth is the connection?
Why would Reina Ryuugu commit an act of violence in a town that had nothing to do with Hinamizawa?
"...To be honest, my head is starting to hurt from all the things I still don't understand."
"Absolutely.@k@vS11/11/420300133. ...In any case, there certainly is something mysterious about Reina Ryuugu.@k@vS11/11/420300134. We have nothing to lose by investigating."
"Indeed.@k@vS11/28/420100615. Sorry to impose, Ooishi-san, but would you mind investigating Reina Ryuugu's alibi for the time of Kouhei Sawamura's death?"
"Understood.@k@vS11/11/420300136. What are you going to do, Tomoe-chan?"
"...This time, I'd like to see this Reina Ryuugu girl with my own eyes.@k@vS11/28/420100617. Is she really the kind of person I've heard about...?"
Tomoe rested her chin on her hand after saying that.
...Reina Ryuugu. And Hinamizawa village.
She wanted to uncover whatever the connection was between the two of them.
